channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: SpaceX has launched a pair of lunar landers for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business up there.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft commits to AI integration, but delivers no particulars to differentiate from rivals
item.description: In his latest AI positioning statement, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said Monday that everything must revolve around Azure. The company is labeling this change, which came with various internal executive shifts, CoreAI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: California pulls back EPA requests for approval to phase out diesel-powered trains and big rigs
item.description: California has withdrawn requests for the EPA to give the state authority to enforce rules limiting emissions from diesel-powered trains and big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A CSO’s perspective: 8 cyber predictions for 2025
item.description: As we step into 2025, the cyberthreat landscape is once again more dynamic and challenging than the year before. In 2024, we witnessed a remarkable acceleration in cyberattacks of all types, many fueled by advancements in generative AI. For security leaders, the stakes are higher than ever.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bannon says he realizes Musk 'not going to be totally out' of MAGA
item.description: Steve Bannon said Tuesday that tech billionaire Elon Musk is “not gonna be totally out” of the MAGA movement, even as the former adviser to President-elect Trump has lobbed criticism at the tech billionaire.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: DJI Mavic 4 Pro leaks show it could improve my favorite camera drone with a redesigned triple camera module
item.description: Leaked images seemingly show the rumored DJI Mavic 4 Pro's redesigned bulbous camera module which could mean improved image quality and range of motion.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Street Fighter 6 gets new gameplay trailer for next DLC character Mai Shiranui
item.description: Capcom has revealed the gameplay trailer for Street Fighter 6's next playable character, Mai Shiranui.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden Signs Executive Order on Artificial Intelligence, Seeks to Ensure Infrastructure Development
item.description: President Joe Biden signs an executive order on artificial intelligence, aiming to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations can be built quickly and at scale in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Next-gen Ethernet standards set to move forward in 2025
item.description: 2024 was a banner year for Ethernet, which has been a foundational element of networking for more than 50 years and is poised for more advancements as 2025 kicks off. Multiple standards and certification efforts made progress in 2024, helping networks to be more reliable and deliver more bandwidth.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Nintendo Sound Clock: Alarmo is coming to participating retailers in March 2025, no Switch Online membership required
item.description: Nintendo has announced that its Sound Clock: Alarmo will be coming to participating retailers in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Skeleton Crew's season 2 chances don't look great, but the Star Wars show's creators are 'happy to make more' if it is renewed
item.description: Skeleton Crew's creators are open to making a second season, but it's unclear if the show will be renewed.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Meta’s fact-checking changes stoke disinformation concerns
item.description: The end of fact-checking at Meta is raising fresh concerns its platforms will become a hotbed of disinformation as the network hands over the policing of content to users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Unauthorized drone flights double; 3 arrested during LA fires
item.description: The FAA instituted temporary flight restrictions in the area. Aircraft are used to drop water on the blazes.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Apple execs head to London to fight for the App Store
item.description: On the day the UK government confirms an AI future for the country (we’ll see how that goes), Apple’s App Store pricing model has gone on trial in London.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech giants join forces to better support Chromium-based browsers
item.description: The Linux Foundation has unveiled a new collaborative organization called Supporters of Chromium-Based Browsers designed to ensure that open-source projects with connections to Chromium get the necessary resources to be successful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China’s newest humanoid robot is ready to serve like never before
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a new bipedal humanoid robot that stands over 5 feet tall and can tackle a wide range of tasks in different settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS ‘prepared’ for 2025 filing season, but potential spending cuts loom for the agency
item.description: For the 2025 tax filing season, the IRS is expanding several modernization efforts. But potential budget cuts on the table could curtail those efforts. The post IRS ‘prepared’ for 2025 filing season, but potential spending cuts loom for the agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Oracle refuses to yield JavaScript trademark, Deno Land says
item.description: JavaScript runtime provider Deno Land’s efforts to get Oracle to yield the trademark for JavaScript have hit a snag, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw the trademark, Deno Land said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: H-1B: Silicon Valley’s favorite foreign-worker visa under attack, while Trump, Musk offer support
item.description: The schism in MAGA raised questions about the fate in the second Trump era of a visa seen in Silicon Valley as key to the technology industry's innovation and success. It comes at a time when the tech industry has gained unprecedented proximity to U.S. levers of power.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Working with minimal APIs in .NET
item.description: If you’re building cloud-native applications with microservices, they need to be small. You don’t want code that comes with enough dependencies and required libraries to turn something that should be able to scale in milliseconds into megabytes of code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia launches blueprints to help jump start AI projects
item.description: Give Nvidia credit, it’s not just talking up big ideas, it’s helping its customers achieve them. The vendor recently issued designs for AI factories after hyping up the idea for several months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
